  <h2 id="lecture_heading" class="section-title dsp-flex-xs flex-align-items-center-xs" data-lecture-id="11233631" data-next-lecture-id="23737529" data-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/11233631" data-next-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/23737529" ,="" data-previous-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/9106068" data-previous-lecture-id="9106068">

    19. WHATSAPP?? Really??
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=2 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=22662783, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-22662783">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p><strong>by </strong><a href="https://www.psychokiller.eu/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"><strong>Tomson Darko</strong></a><strong> (TMS Artist)</strong></p><p>If you're sick of algorithms, shadow-bans or having less and less of your followers and fans see your work... There's a new game in town.</p><p>I'm going to show you how I created a WhatsApp list with 2000 engaged fans... With an average click through rate of 40%!</p><p>Nuts, I know. I mean, look at the data:  </p><p>My Instagram Story clicks... 8% </p><p>Facebook... 15%. </p><p>My email newsletter... around 2%.</p><p>So, what's my WhatsApp formula? </p><p><strong>I send one message a week with a link to my latest blog post.</strong></p><p>That's it.</p><p>The only problem?</p><p>It doesn't scale.</p><p>So if you're lazy. You're gonna hate it.</p><p>With my system you're gonna have to get your hands dirty. But it's definitely worth it.</p><p>Let me show you how. </p><p><strong>WAIT. WHO ARE YOU?</strong></p><p>My name is Tomson Darko. I’m Dutch. And I have a blog called <a href="https://www.psychokiller.eu/" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Psychokiller</a>.</p><p>I publish fictional (short) stories. They're about the darker themes of life like "feeling alone while surrounded by people", "having sex with people you hate" etc.</p><p>When people connect to my work, they show their support by buying my self-published books. That's how I make money. </p><p>Facebook used to be my #1 traffic source back in 2016. I had 25,000 fans at the time. The organic reach was 10,000 unique views per post. Very quickly over the years it dropped down to 2,000 - 3,000 unique views.</p><p>That stung. </p><p>It got harder and harder to reach the fanbase that I had built over the years. Now, every time I published a new story I was reaching less people.  </p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/9033828" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">My email list had 3,000 subscribers, but it was not my mothership</a>. I had ignored it for such a very long time that the open rate was pathetic. </p><p>And then Instagram. 25,000 fans... but I couldn't reach them anymore unless I wanted to pay for ads. Very frustrating. </p><p>So I had to do something.</p><p>The answer was WhatsApp. It's the number one social app in the Netherlands.</p><p><strong>MAKE A PROMISE</strong></p><p>I wondered what would happen if I texted my contacts a link to my latest blog post every Sunday? That was my promise to my contacts. That means that even if I’m on fire and I write 5 posts that week, I still only send that ONE WhatsApp message at the end of the week.</p><p>You have to be specific about what you’re offering to your fans. And when are you going to publish? Once a month? Every day? Every week?</p><p>Pick a frequency and stick to it.</p><p>Pick a content type and stick to it.</p><p>Don’t say just say something like "Subscribe for more inspiration". Say "Subscribe and I'll text you with an inspirational quote every morning". Or "Subscribe and I will send you exclusive behind-the-scenes photos every first Monday of the month.</p><p>And never break your promise. Give them what they opted-in for.  </p><p><strong>BROADCAST LISTS</strong></p><p>And the best way to do it is with WhatsApp's 'broadcast-list'. Using this, you can send a message to 250 of your contacts at a time.</p><p>==</p><p><em>To create a broadcast-list on Android:</em></p><p>Open WhatsApp.</p><p>Go to the Chats screen > More options > New broadcast.</p><p>Tap + or type in contact names to choose recipients from your contact list.</p><p>Tap Done.</p><p>Tap Create.</p><p>==</p><p><em>To create a broadcast-list on an iPhone:</em></p><p>Open WhatsApp.</p><p>Tap the Broadcast Lists button at the top of the Chats screen.</p><p>Tap New List at the bottom of the Broadcast Lists screen.</p><p>Type your contacts' names or click the (+) button to choose from your contact list.</p><p>Tap Create.</p><p>==</p><p><strong>GET A NUMBER SYSTEM</strong></p><p>A broadcast-list can have a maximum of 250 contacts. So I've created 10 broadcast lists on my phone for my weekly "blasts". So that things don't get confusing, I make sure to numbering my contacts according to what list they're on. So if "Jennie" is opting-in for my list, I'm going to save her as "1. Jennie". That way if she's ever wants to opt-out, I know where to find her: list 1. </p><p>I've heard some people complain about the broadcast-list. "Not everyone on the list gets my message", 'I don't think it works'. </p><p>If you're experiencing this, the problem is not WhatsApp. It's your phone. You need a fast processor (min. 2.0 ghz). And you need a minimum of 4GB RAM to text the message to hundreds of people in just a few seconds. </p><p>When I tried it with my last smartphone that had 2GB RAM, it didn't work very well. The only way to be 100% sure that everyone will get your messages, is with a fast phone. </p><p>I'm using a Samsung A7 android, so I can sync my contacts with Gmail. If the phone fails me, I still have my contacts.</p><p><strong>OPTING-IN</strong></p><p>Now... your new follower has to add YOUR number into their contacts too. Otherwise WhatsApp won’t send them your messages. </p><p>You can make a "pre-filled" WhatsApp message for your followers for the opt-in. </p><p>I'm quoting WhatsApp: </p><p><em>"To create your own link with a pre-filled message that will automatically appear in the text field of a chat, use </em><a href="https://wa.me/whatsappphonenumber?text=urlencodedtext" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"><em>https://wa.me/whatsappphonenumber/?text=urlencodedtext</em></a><em> where whatsappphonenumber is a full phone number in international format and URL-encodedtext is the URL-encoded pre-filled message.</em></p><p><a href="https://wa.me/15551234567?text=I'm%20interested%20in%20your%20art" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Example</a></p><p><a href="https://faq.whatsapp.com/en/26000030/?category=5245251" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Using CLICK TO CHAT</a></p><p><strong>OPTING-OUT</strong></p><p>If someone wants to opt-out you have to delete them from the broadcast-list FIRST. Then, delete them from your phone. If you delete them from your phone first, you won't find them on the list but they will still receive your messages and probably get pissed-off. You don't want that so do it right.</p><p><strong>PROMOTING YOUR WHATSAPP LIST</strong></p><p>I promote my list at the end of every blogpost. </p><p>Like this:</p><p>==</p><p><em>Did you like this? Do you want to know when the next post is coming up? I will personally send you a Whatsapp message with info about the blogpost every Sunday. Follow the instructions:</em></p><p><em>1. Add my number 123344566 to your contact list.</em></p><p><em>2. Send me a message called 'party on' with your first name or use this quick-link.</em></p><p><em>3. If you don't want to receive my messages anymore. Just send me a message that says ‘its over’. </em></p><p>==</p><p><strong>PLATFORM FARMING</strong></p><p>A few times a year I promoting my WhatsApp-list on my Facebook and Instagram stories. I get about 100 or so newbies that way.</p><p>One the last two years I've collected 2,000 contacts in my phone.</p><p>You may not like how slow it is. But at least you don't get burnt by algorithm changes.</p><p>Building trust and expanding is a slow process, but it’s worth it.</p><p>When I started selling Psychokiller merchandise my WhatsApp list were the first to buy stuff. Instagram and Facebook? Crickets.</p><p>The followers on my WhatsApp list are my number one fans.</p><p>They trust me.</p><p>They like my art and they give direct and honest feedback. </p><p>"I love this new story" or "You had a type-o in your last post". Real engagement. Not just heart emojis.</p><p>I get about 10 new contacts every week. </p><p>I lose about 4-5 people every month. </p><p><strong>IT'S NOT A CHAT ROOM</strong></p><p>Oh and you should avoid using your broadcast-list like a chat.</p><p>One time I made that mistake. I wrote: "Hi". Then I received a few hundred replies in 10 seconds from people with "hi" back. Rookie mistake.</p><p>Instead make one clear message and say goodbye. Like this:</p><p>==</p><p>"Hi! </p><p>I hope you have a good day. I wrote a post about leaving the house in the morning before your 'booty call' wakes up. This is the link: <a href="http://www.psychokiller.eu" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">www.psychokiller.eu</a></p><p>See you next week!</p><p>-Tomson"</p><p>==</p><p>Some people will reply, but the majority will just take this as a message they don’t have to reply to.</p><p><strong>BUT ALWAYS REPLY</strong></p><p>Always answer your fans. Show them you're human and that you appreciate them when they take the time out to write you. Nurture that relationship.</p><p>Some people might even be surprised by your reply. They may think your WhatsApp is controlled by bots. </p><p>Show them you're not a bot.</p><p><strong>TRUE FANS</strong></p><p>Why does it work so well?</p><p>It's a one to one relationship.</p><p>And when you show up regularly, you build a real connection. That's what your fans want.</p><p>Most people want things to happen overnight, but I've learned that the best methods don't scale.</p><p>Maybe, you won't get everybody to trust you with their number. But when you do find those that say "Yes. Here's my number. You can text me!" Those are the ones that may become fans for life.</p><p></p>
</div>
